findings.evidence: Only characteristic lemma present is elementaryTwoQuotientMk_eq_zero_iff (line 68); sibling squareClass_prod at SquareClassGroup.lean:56-60.
findings.fix: Add elementaryTwoQuotientMk_mul (g h : G) : elementaryTwoQuotientMk (g*h) = elementaryTwoQuotientMk g + elementaryTwoQuotientMk h, plus elementaryTwoQuotientMk_one and an elementaryTwoQuotientMk_prod over a Finset; or bundle elementaryTwoQuotientMk as an additive hom. Annotate the normal-form ones @[simp].
findings.issue: elementaryTwoQuotientMk has no lemma for its interaction with the group operation (no _mul/_one/_prod), so the map cannot be used without unfolding to QuotientAddGroup.mk. The cited sibling SquareClassGroup provides exactly this as squareClass_prod (SquareClassGroup.lean:56), and the Layer-2 target (2-rank via generators) needs the homomorphism property.

findings.evidence: File exposes only elementaryTwoQuotientMk and elementaryTwoQuotientMk_eq_zero_iff (lines 29, 35).
findings.fix: Once the general _mul/_prod lemmas exist, re-export them here (e.g. ClassGroup.elementaryTwoQuotientMk_mul) so the genus-theory consumer can compute mk of a product of classes without unfolding.
findings.issue: ClassGroup.elementaryTwoQuotientMk re-exports the general mk and inherits the same incomplete characterization: no additivity lemma to express an ideal-class image as a sum.

summary: Clean construction with correct simp annotations and complete card/2-rank API, but the named introduction map elementaryTwoQuotientMk lacks the additivity characteristic lemma that its own cited sibling SquareClassGroup provides.
